message:
chore(beads): close ft-iehgn.3 and ft-1u90p.8.7

- ft-iehgn.3: fleet_memory_controller — unified 5 memory subsystems (b6b93b86)
- ft-1u90p.8.7: beta_feedback_loop — controlled rollout with smoothness telemetry (dab602ab)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.

 {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","title":"Publish user-facing release/tuning guidance for different hardware tiers","description":"Provide clear guidance on expected behavior, config knobs, and troubleshooting paths across low/mid/high-end hardware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Publish hardware-tier guidance backed by validated unit/integration/e2e/perf evidence from the resize/reflow program.
2. Guidance must include explicit operational playbooks, rollback/fallback steps, and diagnostics workflows for each tier.
3. Require structured logging/diagnostics artifact references so operators can map symptoms to recommended actions quickly.
4. Validate guidance via scripted end-to-end operator journeys and update docs when discrepancies are discovered.
5. Closure requires auditable documentation package with evidence links and owner sign-off.","notes":"Dependency rationale: user-facing tuning guidance depends on controlled beta results so recommendations reflect observed real-world smoothness and artifact behavior across hardware tiers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.550243Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T03:36:06.172192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging","track-rollout","user-impact","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":334,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","author":"OliveDesert","text":"OliveDesert progress (2026-02-20): drafted user-facing hardware-tier guidance doc at docs/resize-user-facing-release-tuning-guidance-wa-1u90p.8.5.md covering low/mid/high expectations, recommended tuning profiles (Conservative/Balanced/Performance), release channel posture, troubleshooting commands, fallback triggers, and release-notes template. Added cross-link from docs/resize-rollout-plan-wa-1u90p.8.2.md so rollout documentation chain now references this guidance.","created_at":"2026-02-20T01:55:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","title":"Execute final go/no-go review and archive evidence bundle for sign-off","description":"## Context
Final release promotion requires a single authoritative evidence package and decision record.

## Scope
Execute formal go/no-go review using objective evidence from correctness, performance, reliability, and user-perception tracks.

## Deliverables
- Consolidated evidence bundle (unit/integration/e2e/perf/soak outputs)
- Risk register with residual risks and mitigation ownership
- Signed decision record with explicit release rationale and rollback readiness

## Why this matters
Ensures release quality and accountability are evidence-driven, not impression-driven.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence bundle includes pass/fail summaries and links to detailed artifacts for all required tracks.
- No unresolved critical blocker remains; exceptions are explicitly approved and documented.
- Rollback plan, kill-switch verification, and on-call readiness are confirmed.
- Decision record references beta feedback and SLO compliance data.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Complete go/no-go review with an authoritative evidence bundle spanning correctness, performance, reliability, and user-perceived smoothness gates.
2. Require explicit sign-off criteria: no unresolved critical blockers, documented exceptions with approvers, and validated rollback/kill-switch readiness.
3. Require comprehensive validation references: unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end scripts, benchmarks, and soak outcomes tied to release decision thresholds.
4. Require structured diagnostics/logging and artifact index for every evidence source so the decision is fully auditable and reproducible.
5. Closure requires signed decision record, archived artifacts, on-call readiness confirmation, and residual-risk ownership map.
6. Validation must include numeric user-facing SLO thresholds and measured deltas for typing latency, reflow latency, and jitter with explicit pass/fail verdicts.
7. Closure requires at least one deterministic end-to-end script under tests/e2e/rio/ dedicated to this bead’s scenarios, with reproducible invocation instructions.
8. Structured JSONL logs must include correlation identifiers (scenario_id, invariant_id, pathology_id, trace_id) so failures can be traced across unit, integration, and e2e evidence.","notes":"Release-governance control point: this bead is a hard gate, not a summary task. Keep evidence traceability and rollback readiness explicit so operator confidence is data-backed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.654838Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T01:39:57.132921Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T01:39:57.132913Z","close_reason":"Merged into canonical reactivity execution tree ft-2p9cb (see ft-2p9cb.7 no-loss mapping -02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-1u90p.8.7","title":"Run controlled beta feedback loop with user-perceived smoothness telemetry","description":"## Context
Lab metrics alone can miss user-perception failures.

## Scope
Add a structured beta loop combining telemetry and qualitative feedback to validate perceived smoothness and stability before broad rollout.

## Deliverables
- Beta cohort plan and feedback taxonomy
- Telemetry-to-feedback correlation dashboard
- Decision rubric for promotion/rollback between rollout stages

## Why this matters
Ensures release decisions reflect real user outcomes, not just internal benchmark wins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Beta runs collect statistically useful telemetry and categorized feedback.
- Promotion decisions cite both objective SLOs and perception signals.
- Findings are documented and reflected in final release guidance.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Execute controlled beta loop with explicit unit/integration/e2e evidence references tied to user-perceived smoothness and reliability outcomes.
2. Collect structured telemetry and user feedback cohorts with deterministic sampling and reproducible analysis artifacts.
3. Validate rollback/mitigation playbooks via end-to-end test scripts before broad rollout actions are approved.
4. Document anomaly/failure taxonomy with triage owners and close-loop remediation tracking.
5. Closure requires auditable evidence package demonstrating readiness for final go/no-go decisions.","notes":"Dependency rationale: controlled beta is gated by compatibility contract, alt-screen conformance, soak stability, telemetry dashboards, and canary plan so user-facing exposure starts only after high-risk regressions are screened out.

Added preparatory deliverable doc: docs/resize-controlled-beta-feedback-loop-wa-1u90p.8.7.md covering beta cohort plan + feedback taxonomy, telemetry-to-feedback correlation contract, and GO/HOLD/ROLLBACK rubric. Ready to execute final pass once ft-1u90p.7.7 closes.","created_at":"2026-02-18T05:04:23Z"},{"id":336,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"QuietLake execution update (2026-02-22):

Completed in this pass:
1. Reassigned bead to `QuietLake` and moved status to `in_progress` after unblock from `ft-1u90p.7.7` closure.
2. Updated `docs/resize-controlled-beta-feedback-loop-wa-1u90p.8.7.md`:
   - status changed from preparatory/dependency-gated to active/unblocked
   - added current checkpoint section and evidence links
3. Materialized evidence artifacts under `evidence/wa-1u90p.8.7/`:
   - `beta_feedback_log.jsonl`
   - `telemetry_feedback_correlation.csv`
   - `cohort_daily_summary.json`
   - `decision_checkpoint_20260222.md`

Checkpoint outcome for 2026-02-22:
- Decision: `HOLD`
- Rationale: sample sufficiency and real-user feedback thresholds are not yet met.
- Telemetry snapshot sourced from deterministic alt-screen conformance fixture-only run:
  - artifact dir: `e2e-artifacts/2026-02-22T17-53-14Z/scenario_01_alt_screen_conformance/`
  - events_total=66, alt_true=5, alt_false=5, resize_pulse=50, failed_events=0, m1_p95_ms=6, m1_p99_ms=6

Remaining to close bead:
- ingest actual C2 user feedback + telemetry joins across low/mid/high tiers
- satisfy 14-day and threshold criteria documented in the bead/doc before GO/HOLD/ROLLBACK promotion review

 {"id":"ft-1u90p.9","title":"Latency-immunity architecture contract for local-first input/reflow under remote mux pressure","description":"## Mission
Guarantee **local interaction immunity** in FrankenTerm: typing, paste echo, and resize/font-change reflow must remain responsive even when remote mux state is massive and remote hosts are CPU-starved, swapping, or IO-stalled.

## Why This Exists (Problem Statement)
Legacy mux behavior showed a pathological feedback loop:
1. many agents produce heavy output across many PTYs
2. remote mux CPU rises, swap grows, storage pressure adds IO stalls
3. capture + processing latency rises
4. UI-facing interaction (typing/reflow) gets coupled to remote degradation
5. user sees hitching, delayed input, and unstable resize behavior

This bead establishes and operationalizes a **fundamentally different architecture contract**: local-first control-plane decisions with bounded degradation and explicit guardrails.

## Architectural Principles
- Local interaction path is sacred: input and viewport reflow budgets are protected first.
- Remote degradation is isolated, not propagated.
- Background/cold work is the first thing demoted, shed, or deferred.
- Every protection has measurable telemetry and rollback controls.
- No hidden heuristics: invariants, SLOs, and evidence gates are explicit.
- Local sovereignty: when local machine health preconditions hold, user-critical progress cannot be blocked by remote distress.

## Scope
1. Input-preserving backpressure controls (reserve floors, shedding, admission policy).
2. Local snapshot fast path (optimistic reads + safe reclamation).
3. Remote health isolation (SWIM-style suspicion + conservative declaration).
4. Fault-injection + objective GO/HOLD/ROLLBACK evidence pack.
5. Cross-machine pathology elimination with impossible-behavior contracts.
6. Alien reactivity kernel formalism (latency calculus, temporal isolation, verification proofs).
7. Local sovereignty plane leveraging asupersync + frankensqlite primitives.

## Non-Goals
- Cosmetic performance wins that do not protect user-perceived latency.
- Temporary compatibility shims that preserve known architectural debt.
- Releasing without reproducible evidence artifacts.

## Delivery Model
Track `ft-1u90p.9` is decomposed into seven execution tracks (`.9.1` to `.9.7`) with granular subtasks, explicit dependencies, and quality gates so execution remains auditable and parallelizable.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","design":"Latency-immunity architecture = local-first budget governance + remote-isolation controls + deterministic degradation ladder + evidence-driven rollout.

Core pattern:
1. Reserve local interaction budgets before any background/remote work.
2. Keep critical read paths local and low-contention (snapshot fast path).
3. Treat remote health as a classified signal, not a hard coupling point.
4. Validate via fault injection and deterministic replay before promotion.","acceptance_criteria":"1. FrankenTerm remains locally responsive for text entry and text reflow (resize/shape/font-size) when local machine health preconditions are satisfied, even under severe remote mux degradation.
2. Architecture enforces local-first decoupling so remote backlog cannot directly stall input/reflow critical paths.
3. Validation includes comprehensive unit/property/concurrency tests plus integration and deterministic e2e scripts for high-state remote pressure scenarios.
4. Detailed structured logs and machine-readable artifacts are required for all guardrail/isolation/SLI gate verdicts.
5. Release gates fail deterministically on any reproducible sluggishness or missing evidence; heavy campaigns run via `rch exec` wrappers with fallback reporting.
6. User-facing SLO thresholds (typing latency, reflow latency, and jitter) are numerically defined and verified in artifactized validation runs before closure.
7. Execution coordination is mandatory: claim wave, file reservation plan, and handoff artifact links (tests/log manifests) are recorded in bead comments before status transitions.
8. Final GO/HOLD/ROLLBACK dossier includes linked evidence from tracks .9.1 through .9.7, including asupersync/frankensqlite sovereignty-plane validation outcomes.","notes":"Future-self guidance:
- This track exists to prevent regression to "remote pressure causes local sluggishness".
- Prefer explicit invariants over ad hoc tuning; tuning without invariants regresses silently.
- If tradeoffs emerge, preserve local input/reflow first and degrade remote/background throughput.
- Capture all exceptions and edge-case decisions in bead comments to preserve intent continuity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaDesert","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:02:10.694933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:07:38.128221Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:07:38.128160Z","close_reason":"Closure audit complete: all ft-1u90p.9.x child tracks closed; parent contract documented with evidence anchors and residual gate note.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","quality-gate","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":337,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution note: This bead family is intentionally over-documented to be self-contained for future contributors. All child beads include rationale, scope boundaries, acceptance, and artifact expectations so implementation can proceed without reopening prior markdown planning docs.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:30:02Z"},{"id":338,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Plan-space optimization pass (2026-02-22): pruned coarse/transitive dependency edges to increase safe parallelism without removing any causal/safety gates; added explicit unit/integration/e2e+structured-logging requirements where implicit; added ft-1u90p.9.5.12 for local-health precondition + user-perceived SLI gating (keypress-to-visible, reflow-to-stable) and wired it into scorecard/go-no-go path.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:23:48Z"},{"id":339,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Critical-path optimization pass (2026-02-22): moved .9.1/.9.2/.9.3 readiness blockers from ft-1u90p.9.4.2 (campaign definition) to ft-1u90p.9.4.8 (execution/orchestration), and pruned transitive blockers in .9.5/.9.4. This preserves all safety gates while allowing earlier parallel work in campaign design and pathology harness preparation.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:33:08Z"},{"id":340,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Plan-space hardening pass complete (OrangeBear): audited all open ft-1u90p beads (50 total) for scope quality, dependency coherence, and validation rigor using br+bv.\n\nChanges made:\n- Strengthened acceptance criteria on 14 high-leverage beads to explicitly require comprehensive unit/property/concurrency tests, deterministic e2e scripts, structured JSONL logging with scenario/invariant/pathology traceability, and mandatory user-facing SLO + rch offload/fallback evidence.\n- Elevated parent-track gates on ft-1u90p.9/.9.1/.9.2/.9.3/.9.5 to include explicit numeric typing/reflow/jitter SLO verification and heavy-run offload/fallback metadata requirements.\n\nPost-change audit results:\n- 50/50 open ft-1u90p beads now include unit + e2e + detailed logging expectations in plan text.\n- Parent track gates now uniformly encode user-SLO and offload evidence requirements.\n- No feature scope removed; changes are stricter closure contracts only.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:34:22Z"},{"id":341,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution wave + reservation matrix (optimization pass):
Wave A (parallel, low file collision): ft-1u90p.9.2.1, ft-1u90p.9.3.1, ft-1u90p.9.4.1, ft-1u90p.9.5.1.
Wave B (implementation): .9.1 lane first (3->4->5) with strict hotspot ownership to avoid scheduler-file collisions; .9.2 and .9.3 lanes run parallel where reservations do not overlap.
Wave C (validation): ft-1u90p.9.4.8 + ft-1u90p.9.5.12 + ft-1u90p.9.5.13 orchestrate deterministic campaign/e2e/soak evidence.
Wave D (gating): ft-1u90p.9.4.4 -> ft-1u90p.9.4.5 -> ft-1u90p.9.4.7.
Reservation policy: before claim, post exact path patterns and expected handoff artifact IDs in bead comment; if a hotspot file is shared, only one active owner at a time.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:36:03Z"},{"id":1190,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Radical architecture note (2026-02-23): local sovereignty is now explicitly modeled as a first-class objective across .9.1-.9.7. The user-facing requirement is strict: local typing/reflow responsiveness must remain fast even during remote CPU+swap+IO meltdown regimes.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:24Z"},{"id":1472,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","author":"MagentaDesert","text":"Closure audit complete (2026-02-25): all child tracks ft-1u90p.9.1 through ft-1u90p.9.7 and their granular beads are closed. Updated docs/latency-immunity-architecture-ft-1u90p.9.md status to closure-audited and added explicit evidence anchors + residual external gate note for ft-1u90p.8.7 (still HOLD/in_progress in rollout track). This parent architecture contract is complete and ready to close.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:07:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","title":"Input-preserving backpressure hardening (reserve floors + S3-FIFO admission)","description":"## Track Charter: Input-Preserving Backpressure Hardening

## Objective
Implement a deterministic backpressure model where local interaction work keeps budget priority under overload, with explicit reserve floors, dynamic surge handling, and controlled shedding of non-critical background work.

## Background
The observed failure mode is not just "too much work"; it is **wrong work ordering** under pressure. This track enforces strict ordering so local interaction and viewport reflow remain responsive while non-critical tasks absorb pressure.

## Scope
1. Formalize budget invariants and starvation constraints.
2. Implement reserve floor and surge-reserve behavior in scheduler accounting.
3. Add background shedding + starvation escape hatch.
4. Integrate smarter admission/eviction policy (S3-FIFO-inspired where applicable).
5. Expose stale/drop markers and diagnostics for operators.
6. Validate via unit/property/integration/e2e stress scenarios.

## Out of Scope
- Rewriting unrelated rendering subsystems.
- Throughput-only optimization that harms interaction latency.

## Definition of Done
All guardrail and shedding behavior is deterministic, observable, tested, and reversible via feature flags/kill-switches.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Local interaction budgets remain protected under synthetic remote pressure.
2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover reserve arithmetic, shedding triggers, and starvation escape invariants.
3. Integration and e2e scenarios prove no local input starvation during remote stall windows.
4. Detailed structured logs surface reserve usage, shedding events, starvation escapes, and decision causality.
5. Rollout/rollback controls are documented and verified, with heavy suites run via rch offload wrappers where applicable.
6. User-facing SLO thresholds (typing latency, reflow latency, and jitter) must be numerically defined and verified in artifactized validation runs before closure.
7. Execution coordination is mandatory: claim wave, file reservation plan, and handoff artifact links (tests/log manifests) must be recorded in bead comments before status transitions.","notes":"Reasoning:
- This is the first implementation lane because other lanes assume these guardrails exist.
- Without this lane, snapshot and remote-health improvements can still regress interaction latency.
- Keep this lane conservative and reversible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:44.612051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-24T07:47:11.777486Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T07:47:11.777465Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","quality-gate","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":342,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Track intent: enforce 'local interaction first' ordering under overload. If tradeoffs appear, preserve input/reflow latency and shed background throughput.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:30:03Z"},{"id":343,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Lane ownership rule: ft-1u90p.9.1.3 and ft-1u90p.9.1.4 are expected to touch shared scheduler hotspots; default to serialized ownership unless path reservations prove disjoint. Handoff artifact required before moving owner: unit/property results + structured log sample + updated config/metric key map.","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:36:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.9.1.1","title":"Define latency-immunity invariants and SLO mapping for input-preserving backpressure","description":"## Objective
Codify the invariant contract for local-first backpressure so later implementation tasks cannot drift into heuristic-only behavior.

## Background / Why
Without explicit invariants, tuning changes can unintentionally re-couple local responsiveness to remote pressure. This task establishes the contract that all `.9.1` implementation must satisfy.

## Scope
1. Define hard invariants for input reserve floor, resize floor, and starvation escape behavior.
2. Define SLO mappings (p50/p95/p99 interaction latency and frame budgets).
3. Define metric names and units required for guardrail observability.
4. Define failure taxonomy and expected scheduler responses.

## Deliverables
- Invariant matrix for scheduler decisions.
- SLO-to-metric mapping table.
- Failure taxonomy with expected degradation actions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invariants are explicit, non-contradictory, and testable.
- Every planned guardrail behavior has telemetry mapping.
- Downstream tasks can reference this bead as normative source.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Invariant spec defines explicit numeric floors for input/resize budgets and starvation-escape maxima.
2. Unit/property test mapping is specified for each invariant with reproducible scenario IDs.
3. Integration/e2e validation paths are defined for typing, paste, resize, and reflow under pressure.
4. Structured logging schema for scheduler decisions is complete and unambiguous.
5. Downstream beads reference this contract as normative source.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:30:42.906563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:52:07.348020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T22:52:07.348007Z","close_reason":"Normative invariants/SLO contract completed; restored closed state after planning pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","logging","performance","reflow","resize","test-coverage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":344,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Future-self note: this bead is normative for . If implementation conflicts with this invariant set, update this bead first rather than patching behavior ad hoc.","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:31:06Z"}]}
